metadata.title: AI Tools Knowledge Graph
metadata.description: A comprehensive knowledge graph of AI tools, methodologies, patterns, and best practices for agentic AI development and Claude Code workflows.
metadata.purpose: Indexed reference for AI agents and developers to quickly discover tools, techniques, and architectural patterns.

categories.name: Claude Code Ecosystem
categories.description: Tools and techniques for Claude Code IDE and extensions

categories.name: LLM Debugging & Testing
categories.description: Tools for testing and debugging AI agent behavior

categories.name: AI Marketing
categories.description: AI applications for marketing and branding

nodes.name: Claude Code
nodes.description: IDE for Claude providing comprehensive agentic coding capabilities with plan mode, file operations, and terminal access.
best_practices: Use plan mode for complex tasks

nodes.name: Everything Claude Code
nodes.description: Comprehensive guide repository extending Claude Code functionality with 10+ GitHub repositories of curated techniques.

nodes.name: Awesome Claude Code
nodes.description: Curated list of Claude Code resources, extensions, tips, and community-contributed patterns.

nodes.name: GSD (Get Shit Done)
nodes.description: Agentic orchestration framework that implements plan mode and subagent delegation patterns for complex multi-step workflows.

nodes.name: Ruflo (Claude Flow)
nodes.description: Lightweight CLI framework for building workflows on top of Claude Code. Provides workflow orchestration primitives.

nodes.name: VoltAgent
nodes.description: Multi-agent framework that extends Claude Code with agent capabilities and coordination patterns.

nodes.name: Paperclip
nodes.description: Alternative workflow architecture to Ruflo, offering different approach to Claude Code integration and automation.

nodes.name: Talc AI
nodes.description: Debugging and testing framework for Claude Code providing runtime inspection and error analysis capabilities.

nodes.name: Claude Code Ultimate Guide
nodes.description: Comprehensive educational guide covering Claude Code usage, context management, and advanced patterns.

nodes.name: Awesome Claude Toolkit
nodes.description: Collection of MCP (Model Context Protocol) integrations and tools for extending Claude Code functionality.

nodes.name: Claude Loom Templates
nodes.description: Template library for Claude Code providing pre-built patterns and prompt structures.

nodes.name: Awesome Claude Skills
nodes.description: Curated repository of Claude Code skills, techniques, and capability demonstrations.

nodes.name: GSD Build Tool
nodes.description: Build and deployment tool within GSD ecosystem for automating project builds and deployments.

nodes.name: Claude Subagents Pattern
nodes.description: Architectural pattern for delegating work to specialized sub-agents, improving task accuracy and modularity.

nodes.name: Claude Code Templates
nodes.description: Pre-built starting templates for common Claude Code project patterns and workflows.

nodes.name: Plan Mode
nodes.description: Core Claude Code feature enabling agents to create detailed execution plans before implementation, improving success rates and reducing iterations.
nodes.workflow: 1. Analyze task → 2. Create plan → 3. Execute → 4. Validate
nodes.best_practice: Always use plan mode for tasks with >3 steps

nodes.name: Subagent Delegation
nodes.description: Pattern for breaking complex tasks into specialized sub-agents, each handling specific responsibilities with focused context.
nodes.workflow: 1. Main agent analyzes task → 2. Creates subagent for each responsibility → 3. Subagents execute in parallel → 4. Results aggregated
nodes.best_practice: Create 2-4 focused subagents per complex task

nodes.name: Context Management
nodes.description: Techniques for efficiently managing Claude Code context window to maximize usable tokens for the task at hand.
nodes.best_practice: Reserve 20% of context for task execution, use rest for reference

nodes.name: Test-Driven Development
nodes.description: Enforce RED-GREEN-REFACTOR cycle: delete any code written before a failing test exists.
nodes.workflow: 1. Write failing test → 2. Implement to pass → 3. Refactor → Repeat

nodes.name: MCP (Model Context Protocol) Integration
nodes.description: Extend Claude Code with external tools, APIs, and data sources through standardized MCP servers.
nodes.best_practice: 2-3 core MCPs globally, project-specific ones in .claude/mcp.json

nodes.name: Hooks for Workflow Automation
nodes.description: Scripts triggered before/after events (file edits, commits, deployments) to automate repetitive tasks.

nodes.name: Learn Claude Code
nodes.description: Educational repository with progressive learning path for Claude Code, from basics to advanced patterns.
